# RFC-0002: Performance-Improvement Opportunity Loop for Server-Arg Search

> **Version:** 0.1.1 | **Status:** normative | **Phase:** spec

**References:** [RFC-0001:C-OBJECTIVE](../rfc/RFC-0001.md#rfc-0001c-objective), [RFC-0001:C-MEASUREMENT](../rfc/RFC-0001.md#rfc-0001c-measurement)

---

## 1. Summary

### [RFC-0002:C-SUMMARY] Summary (Informative) <a id="rfc-0002c-summary"></a>

This RFC specifies a loop that turns the ranked configuration frontier produced under
[RFC-0001](../rfc/RFC-0001.md) into identified, actionable performance-improvement opportunities.

**Scope:** How optimization effort is directed (developer inspection of the top-ranked
configurations), the requirement to profile a configuration before proposing a change to
it, and the rule that a code change invalidates and requires re-benchmarking the affected
rankings.

**Out of scope:** Automated headroom, roofline, or reference-gap estimation of a
configuration's optimization ceiling; and the implementation of any specific
optimization.

**Rationale:** The search in [RFC-0001](../rfc/RFC-0001.md) selects the best configuration under the
current code. This RFC adds the feedback path by which the code itself is improved, while
deliberately avoiding automated ceiling prediction, which is not reliable enough to act
on.

*Since: v0.1.0*

---

## 2. Specification

### [RFC-0002:C-OPPORTUNITY-SOURCE] Opportunity input is the ranked frontier (Normative) <a id="rfc-0002c-opportunity-source"></a>

The opportunity loop MUST take the ranked decode-throughput-versus-decode-latency Pareto
frontier defined by [RFC-0001:C-OBJECTIVE](../rfc/RFC-0001.md#rfc-0001c-objective) as its input. Optimization effort MUST be
directed by inspecting the top-ranked configurations on that frontier. The loop MUST NOT
rely on automated headroom, roofline, or reference-gap scoring to predict a
configuration's optimization ceiling or to re-prioritize lower-ranked configurations.

**Rationale:** The measured frontier is the trustworthy prioritization signal; a developer
reviewing the top ranks is the effective way to spot real opportunities."""

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0002:C-PROFILE] Profile before proposing an optimization (Normative) <a id="rfc-0002c-profile"></a>

Before an optimization is proposed for a configuration, that configuration MUST be
profiled at its frontier operating point(s) to attribute time to kernels or execution
phases. The profile attribution MUST be recorded alongside the proposed optimization, and
the proposal MUST name the specific kernel or phase it targets.

**Rationale:** Profiling localizes the bottleneck so that engineering effort targets a
measured hotspot rather than a guess; recording the attribution and the named target makes
the expected effect checkable against a post-optimization re-benchmark.

*Since: v0.1.0*

### [RFC-0002:C-RANK-VALIDITY] Rankings are revision-scoped (Normative) <a id="rfc-0002c-rank-validity"></a>

A frontier ranking is valid only for the code revision recorded in its provenance (per
[RFC-0001:C-MEASUREMENT](../rfc/RFC-0001.md#rfc-0001c-measurement)). Any code or kernel change that affects a measured execution
path MUST invalidate the rankings of all configurations that exercise that path, and
those configurations MUST be re-benchmarked before the ranking is reused.

**Rationale:** An optimization can reorder the frontier — a previously lower-ranked
configuration may surpass the prior best once its bottleneck is removed — so a ranking
cannot be carried across code changes.

*Since: v0.1.0*

---
